name: python-lib

applies_to.description: Python library project with src/ layout, tests/ mirror, and pyproject.toml at root. Suitable for packages intended for distribution via PyPI or internal reuse as a library. Conventional structure: src/<pkg>/__init__.py, tests/test_*.py, docs/ for documentation, scripts/ for CI/utility helpers.

default_rules.note: Python source files under src/ are at their canonical location; the src/ layout is the authoritative package root for this template
Test files already under tests/ are canonically placed; no action needed

Python test files not already under tests/ should be relocated there; stray test files often accumulate at root or in src/ during exploratory development — ASK_USER if the file appears to be a utility script that happens to have test_ prefix without pytest imports
Markdown documentation already under docs/ is canonically placed; no action needed
Shell scripts already under scripts/ are canonically placed; no action needed
Shell scripts not already under scripts/ should be moved there; python-lib projects typically centralize all CI/utility scripts in scripts/
Standard root-level documentation files are canonically at project root; must evaluate before the loose-markdown rule t8 to prevent misrouting
Loose markdown at project root not matched by t7 (README, CHANGELOG, etc.) should move to docs/; this catches draft docs, design notes, and one-off memos that accumulate at root
Files whose basename begins with 'tmp' or 'scratch' are temporary work artefacts with no long-term value; quarantine to .delete — always ASK_USER before ratifying .delete moves

Numbered note/fix files (notes1.md, fix3.md) are historical threads that have accumulated over time; archive rather than delete — ASK_USER-worthy because author may have attachment to specific entries; FLAG_NEW_RULE if this pattern is recurring

notes: Standard python-lib template. Assumes src/ layout (src/mypkg/__init__.py) — the conventional layout for PyPI-distributed libraries. If the project uses flat layout (mypkg/ at root instead of src/mypkg/), rule t1 pattern should be changed to '<pkg_name>/**/*.py'. Rule t3 (**/test_*.py → tests/) is intentionally broad — TRIAGE should check for pytest imports before confirming; utility files near tests/ that lack pytest imports should stay IN_PLACE. The ignore_paths list covers all standard Python tooling caches; users may need to add project-specific build output paths.
